L. 98–52, title I, § 10897 Stat. 285July 29, 1958July 15, 1983In paragraph (1)(A), the word “Earth’s” is capitalized for consistency in title 51.

children.label: A
children.text: research into, and the solution of, problems of flight within and outside the Earth’s atmosphere;

children.label: B
children.text: the development, construction, testing, and operation for research purposes of aeronautical and space vehicles;

children.label: C
children.text: the operation of a space transportation system including the space shuttle, upper stages, space platforms, and related equipment; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other activities as may be required for the exploration of space.

children.heading: .—Aeronautical and space vehicles
children.label: 2 .—Aeronautical and space vehicles
children.text: The term “aeronautical and space vehicles” means aircraft, missiles, satellites, and other space vehicles, manned and unmanned, together with related equipment, devices, components, and parts.
